title: Mega Glimmora Battle Guide
summary: A [Rock]/[Poison] special attacker with [adaptability] — STAB is doubled, 150 Special Attack is massive, and it still sets [stealth-rock].
weaknesses: No 4x Ground weakness thanks to [Poison] typing

recommendedItems.reason: Mandatory Mega Stone with no alternative

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
Mega Glimmora is a [Rock]/[Poison] special attacker that keeps its core identity as a hazard setter while gaining [adaptability] to double its STAB damage. 150 Special Attack, 101 Speed, and two STAB moves getting a 2x boost make it one of the most pressuring hazard leads in the format.

## Strengths
Under [adaptability], [power-gem] becomes an effective ~160 BP [Rock] STAB and [sludge-bomb] hits just as hard for [Fairy] and [Grass] coverage. 101 Speed outruns most natural-speed attackers, and setting [stealth-rock] on turn one is still a valid play because of how threatening the attacking moveset is.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Ground], [Water], [Psychic], and [Steel] are all active weaknesses. Thankfully the [Poison] half removes the 4x [Ground] weakness that its [Rock] typing would normally imply. 83/105/96 bulk is average — survives one neutral hit but not a super-effective one.

## Recommended Moveset
[power-gem] is the main STAB. [sludge-bomb] is the secondary STAB with a poison proc. [stealth-rock] maintains its hazard role. [earth-power] covers [Steel] walls like [heatran] and [ferrothorn].

## Item · Ability · Tera
[glimmorite] is mandatory. [adaptability] activates post-Mega. Tera [Poison] stacks on [sludge-bomb] and grants a [Ground] immunity; Tera [Rock] boosts [power-gem] instead.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Grass] and [Flying] partners cover [Ground] and [Water]. [Dark] or [Bug] partners cover [Psychic]. [tailwind] extends the sweeping window.

## Play Patterns
Lead with Mega Glimmora, Mega evolve, and set [stealth-rock] on a predicted switch. Follow up with [power-gem] and [sludge-bomb] spam, pivoting to [earth-power] against [Steel] walls. Tera on the turn you commit to pressuring — the double STAB damage is usually a KO.
